pages.title: Welcome to Event Horizon
pages.subtitle: Let's get you set up. First, pick the project folder you want to track. The wizard will create a .flux/ (or .flux-store/) directory automatically if it doesn't exist. You can also run event-horizon init manually as an alternative.

pages.title: What you can do
pages.subtitle: A quick tour of (just some of) what Event Horizon brings to your workflow.

pages.title: Choose your storage mode
pages.subtitle: Pick how Event Horizon stores your tickets. You can change this later in Settings.

pages.title: Pick your AI assistant
pages.subtitle:
Event Horizon installs a workflow skill into your AI coding assistant so it can manage tickets automatically.
(Currently only Claude Code is fully tested and supported)

pages.title: Install the integration
pages.subtitle: This copies the Event Horizon workflow skill into your workspace so the agent knows how to manage your tickets.

pages.title: Add to PATH
pages.subtitle: Run event-horizon from any terminal without typing its full path.

pages.title: Explore the docs
pages.subtitle: Event Horizon ships with built-in documentation covering workflow setup, ticket management, and the agent integration. Worth a quick look before diving in.

pages.title: Import from your project
pages.subtitle: Let's check if your project has docs or tasks we can import.

pages.title: You're all set!
pages.subtitle: Your workspace is ready. Create your first ticket, assign it to your AI assistant, and watch it take off.
